title: Looking for feedback & ideas
author: Delicious-Return4888
selftext:
I’ve been working on a private automated trading system for MetaTrader 5. It’s not just a simple moving average crossover – it’s a multi-engine setup that handles:

**ТHIS  RESULTS IS ON DEMO ACCOUNT NOT WITH REAL MONEY ! !  !**

* Market structure analysis (BOS, CHOCH, FVG, order blocks)
* Liquidity sweep detection with confirmation logic
* Dynamic risk & position sizing (with safety checks)
* Multiple strategy modes (SMC, volume impulses, range breaks, mean reversion)
* Real-time performance tracking + trade logging
* A full dark-mode UI for monitoring and control

The bot runs 24/5, manages partial closes, trailing stops, time-based exits, and even includes optional Telegram alerts and a Monte Carlo robustness module.

Right now it’s in live testing on a demo account. I’m curious – for those who’ve built or used similar systems:

* What’s the most unexpected issue you ran into after going live?
* Any creative ways you handle news events or low-volatility periods?
* How do you balance between signal quality and number of trades?

Not looking to share code or sell anything – just genuinely interested in trading system design discussions. What new things can I add/change?

Thanks in advance!

comments.author: Opening-Berry-6041
comments.body: dude your system sounds wild can you explain how you integrated the Monte Carlo robustness module with the liquidity sweep detection so seamlessly?

replies.author: Delicious-Return4888
replies.body: Monte Carlo runs offline on historical trade profits from the Performance Engine, while liquidity sweep detection stays completely independent in real-time  they never directly interact, which keeps the integration :)

replies.author: Delicious-Return4888
replies.body: Anyway, if the system were seamlessly I wouldn't be asking for advice and improvements here, have a nice day!

comments.author: Just_Technology_8815
comments.body: Hey, that's a really interesting project you're working on. Given the news events, I'd just add an option to suspend trading during those news events—say, for about 20 minutes, for example.

comments.author: Far-Tone886
comments.body:
I think the direction makes sense, especially the idea of having AI present during the actual trading process instead of separate from it.

The part I’d question is this though. A lot of tools already give “advice” or directional bias, but traders don’t really struggle with access to information. It’s more about knowing what to trust and when not to act.

That gap between suggestion and actual execution is where most tools fall apart.

I’ve been exploring more structured AI trading for this, especially with multi pair setups. You can set strategies and risk rules, and it handles execution while showing the reasoning behind each trade. You can also add rules like pausing after drawdowns or loss streaks.

What made a difference for me wasn’t just having an AI to talk to, but seeing how it behaves under constraints and over time.

Curious how you’re thinking about that side. Is the AI just advising, or actually accountable to rules and outcomes?
